Container.Name: manga-tracker
Container.Overview: A self-hosted manga chapter tracking dashboard. Searches and pulls rich metadata from MangaBaka (AniList, MAL, Kitsu, MangaUpdates aggregated). Tracks chapter releases in real-time via the MangaUpdates releases feed. Sends new-chapter push notifications via Pushover. Stores everything locally in SQLite — no external database required.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port to access the Manga Tracker web interface. Navigate to http://[UnraidIP]:[Port] after starting.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the SQLite database. All your tracked series, notifications, and settings are stored here. Back this up to preserve your library.

Config.Name: MangaBaka API Token
Config.Description: Your personal access token from mangabaka.org. Required for searching series and fetching metadata. Get one free at https://mangabaka.org.

Config.Name: Pushover User Key
Config.Description: [Optional] Your Pushover user key. Leave blank to configure via the Settings page in the web UI instead. Pushover requires a one-time $5 purchase at https://pushover.net.

Config.Name: Pushover App Token
Config.Description: [Optional] The API token for your Pushover application. Create an app at https://pushover.net/apps and paste the token here, or configure it in the Settings UI.

Config.Name: Update Poll Interval (hours)
Config.Description: How often the background job checks MangaUpdates and MangaBaka for new chapters. Default is every 6 hours. MangaUpdates rate limits are generous so values as low as 1 are fine.

Config.Name: Allowed Origins
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of origins allowed to reach the API. Must include the URL you use to open the web UI, e.g. http://192.168.1.100:8765. If left blank, only localhost is allowed and the web UI will not load.

Config.Name: Database Path (inside container)
Config.Description: [Advanced] Full path inside the container where the SQLite database file is stored. Only change this if you have a specific reason to — it must be inside the Data Directory mount.
